classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council

title: Canon XXIII.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: That no one, whether bishop, presbyter, or deacon, when giving the immaculate Communion, shall exact from him who communicates fees of any kind.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: For grace is not to be sold, nor do we give the sanctification of the Holy Spirit for money; but to those who are worthy of the gift it is to be communicated in all simplicity.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: But if any of those enrolled among the clergy make demands on those he communicates let him be deposed, as an imitator of the error and wickedness of Simon.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Whoever shall demand an obolus or anything else for giving the spotless communion shall be deposed.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: This canon is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
